source: fresh local Korean ASR transcript plus corrected English translation for video 1

entries.text: Hello, I am Kim Jeong Ah, a nurse practitioner working at Eminent Hospice Care.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: Today, I will talk about the services provided by hospice.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: Hospice is a service provided by Medicare according to the Medicare Hospice Benefit.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: On screen: Medicare Hospice Benefit information. Please go to the address on the screen to see the most accurate and detailed information.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: I will talk only about the most general situations among them.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: If you do not have Medicare, you can apply for hospice through Medi-Cal or private insurance.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: Benefits differ a little by insurance, so you need to check your insurance benefits.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: In general, every insurance follows the Medicare Hospice Benefit guidelines.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: On screen: Medicare Hospice Core Services. First, I will talk about Medicare hospice core services.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: Hospice care core services are the services Medicare requires hospice to provide.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: They are doctor, nurse, social worker, spiritual counselor, and chaplain services.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: So each patient must be cared for by these four staff members.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: The remaining visiting staff are optional services a hospice company may provide depending on the patient's needs.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: On screen: Medical doctor. First, I will talk about physicians.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: A hospice patient has two doctors: the patient's original attending physician and the hospice physician.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: So all medications and treatments provided to a hospice patient are ordered by the attending physician or the hospice doctor.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: When a patient starts hospice, in many cases the original attending physician steps back and lets the hospice physician see the patient.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: Depending on the attending physician's opinion, or at the patient's or family's request, the original attending physician can continue caring for the patient.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: The doctor receives reports from the nurse about the patient's current symptoms and orders medications and treatment methods.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: After death, the doctor is involved in writing the death certificate.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: At the six-month point, a doctor or nurse practitioner usually visits in person or by phone
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: to decide whether it is appropriate for the patient to continue receiving hospice services.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: On screen: Nurse, RN or LVN service. Next is the nurse service.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: Depending on the patient's condition, nurses visit once or twice a week, or whenever the patient urgently needs a visit.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: They check whether the patient's current symptoms are being managed well.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: An RN is required to visit at least once every two weeks.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: The nurse monitors the patient's symptoms and continuously follows whether the current nursing and treatment plans are managing those symptoms well.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: If needed, the nurse educates caregivers, reports to the doctor, and helps stop medications or adjust medications that should be added.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: The nurse also refills the patient's current medications, supplies, or medical equipment
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: and helps provide additional items the patient needs.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: One question families often ask is, 'How long does the nurse stay when the nurse comes?'
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: On the nurse's first visit, the nurse looks at every part of the patient and reviews the medications.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: Because the nurse must understand many things, including the caregiving family's environment, the first visit takes a long time.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: After the first visit, you can think of most regular visits as taking around thirty minutes.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: If there are many things that need to be done for the patient,
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: in other words, if the patient has many uncomfortable symptoms, the visit can take longer.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: I would like to briefly give examples of common symptoms hospice patients often experience.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: They include pain, constipation, nausea, vomiting, poor appetite, shortness of breath, infection, and pressure sores.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: One of the many reasons Korean patients receive hospice
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: is dementia.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: Some dementia patients do not have these symptoms,
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: and some may have only one or two of them.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: Or, depending on a specific disease, certain symptoms may become worse.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: For cancer patients, all of the symptoms listed above may appear.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: All the symptoms I mentioned above are symptoms hospice helps manage.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: So if you meet an experienced nurse, or meet a good hospice, you can receive better care.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: They can quickly catch what the patient needs,
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: and among nurses, there are nurses who truly care for patients with attention and love.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: Regardless of knowledge alone, there are nurses who want to do anything more when a patient is suffering.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: Then, in the process of trying to resolve the symptoms the patient has,
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: they find methods, and I have often seen good results.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: For one extreme example, there was a patient with colon cancer.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: When that patient started hospice, the patient had pain, poor appetite, nausea, vomiting, and constipation.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: At first, we adjusted constipation medication step by step, and the constipation was relieved.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: Only the constipation was relieved,
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: but the other symptoms also improved dramatically.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: The patient became much more comfortable than when first admitted
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: and later passed peacefully at the end of life.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: In fact, not only colon cancer patients, but many hospice patients suffer from constipation.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: Older patients eat less and cannot drink water well.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: They are often lying down.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: When they take this medicine and that medicine, especially narcotic pain medication,
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: constipation often occurs.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: So I tell our nurses,
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: during patient visits, even if it is difficult, always check whether stool is impacted
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: by inserting a finger into the anus and confirming it.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: If constipation symptoms improve,
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: many other symptoms are also likely to improve.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: On screen: Social worker service. Now I will talk about the social worker.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: The social worker provides emotional support
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: and provides and discusses information about social welfare services such as Medicare and Medi-Cal.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: For example, in California,
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: they help with applications for Medi-Cal, body donation, IHSS,
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: free meal services, and similar services.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: On screen: Spiritual counselor or chaplain service. Next I will talk about the spiritual counselor.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: This is called a spiritual counselor, and it is usually handled by a pastor or chaplain.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: They visit once a month, or visit regularly,
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: and counsel and care for the spiritual difficulties patients and families are experiencing.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: Many families like our pastor,
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: so sometimes they ask us to send the pastor more often instead of the nurse.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: Bereavement care service for families after death
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: is also handled by the spiritual counselor.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: After the patient's death,
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: the spiritual counselor cares for and comforts the grief experienced by the bereaved family,
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: and helps support them through a difficult time.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: We also provide a service that sends letters for one year.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: The four services I have discussed so far
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: are the hospice core services set by Medicare.
entries.notes: Corrected against fresh Korean ASR transcript; requires final fluent human approval.

entries.text: On screen: Optional services. Next, examples of other optional services are the bath aide and volunteer.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: A bath aide usually visits once or twice a week and provides bathing services.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: For volunteers, if a family wants it, we can send someone once or twice a week for one or two hours.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: They keep lonely patients company or help tired family caregivers take a short rest.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Next are additional services hospice provides that are not care-staff visits.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: On screen: Medications and medical equipment. Hospice provides medications and medical equipment.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Hospice provides the medications patients need and the treatment supplies needed for care.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: For example, for pressure-sore treatment, hospice provides gauze, saline, and ointment.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: For patients who toilet in bed, hospice provides incontinence supplies, diapers, chux, wipes, gloves, and similar items.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: For patients who cannot eat well, hospice provides Ensure or Glucerna to help supplement nutrition.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: On screen: Medical equipment service. Next is medical equipment, sent so the patient can be comfortable and the family can care for the patient more easily.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Hospital beds, overbed tables, air-pressure mattresses, wheelchairs, walkers, oxygen supplies, suction machines, nebulizers, and other items are sent through the hospice rental company according to the patient's needs.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Usually, without hospice, receiving medical equipment requires a doctor's signature, paperwork, and other complicated steps, but hospice patients can receive items simply without that complicated process.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: A hospital bed is especially helpful to the patient, and it is also helpful to the family caring for the patient.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Sometimes people dislike hospital beds, but hospital beds can be adjusted up and down.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Because the head can be raised, we recommend a hospital bed when patients have shortness of breath, poor digestion, or need to eat in bed.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: On screen: Hospice on-call service. Finally, I want to talk about the hospice on-call service.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: It is called emergency triage service; in Korean, it is a standby or waiting service.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Hospice is called a 24/7 service, meaning someone is waiting and answering calls twenty-four hours a day.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: At our company, office hours are from nine to five, and after that Arin answers calls and explains emergency measures.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: When we receive a call from family or facility staff about an emergency, we instruct them on how to respond.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Hospice patients often already have medications or equipment prepared for emergency situations.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: If the situation cannot be handled by phone, a hospice staff member visits the patient directly and provides the needed care.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Even when it is not an emergency, families can call to ask about medication refills or care questions.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Personally, this twenty-four-hour on-call standby service is one of the hardest parts of being a hospice nurse, but it is truly useful for patients and families.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: That is because an emergency can happen to the patient at any time.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: A patient may pass away at ten at night or three in the morning, and we visit, pronounce death, and take the necessary post-death steps.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Because most families are experiencing a death for the first time, simply having hospice staff with them after death helps greatly.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: The family feels reassured, the death and later procedures are completed smoothly, and they become satisfied with hospice service.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: In our hospice, many patients are in nursing homes or care facilities, and we often hear that people like that our company answers the phone well.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Many hospice companies use an answering service company.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Then, after office hours, when a patient or family calls the hospice company, the answering service company receives the call and message.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: When that message is passed to hospice on-call, the on-call hospice nurse judges the situation and calls back.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Korean patients and families often struggle because they must leave a message in English and wait for the call back during an urgent situation.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: At our company, Arin, who speaks English and Korean, answers the phone, so communication is convenient and fast, and patient and family satisfaction is very high.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Thank you, everyone, for watching this video.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: What I talked about today is the general case.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Depending on the patient's and family's situation, and depending on where the patient is staying, the services provided may vary little by little.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Those watching this video may be family members directly caring for a hospice patient, or nurses and medical professionals interested in hospice.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Hospice is holistic care, whole-person nursing.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: It provides care not only for the patient's symptoms, but also for the physical, mental, spiritual, and surrounding environment, together with the family.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: We believe we are doing precious work by protecting the final moments of one life.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Thank you for your hard work in 2024.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Happy New Year in 2025.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.

entries.text: Thank you.
entries.notes: Manually translated from fresh Korean ASR transcript after machine translation collapsed.
